skill.name: web-accessibility
skill.description: Implement web accessibility (a11y) standards following WCAG 2.1 guidelines. Use when building accessible UIs, fixing accessibility issues, or ensuring compliance with disability standards. Handles ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation, screen readers, semantic HTML, and accessibility testing.
skill.summary: Implement web accessibility (a11y) standards following WCAG 2.1 guidelines. Use when building access...
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: All 113 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill is documentation about web accessibility (WCAG 2.1). The scanner detected backticks in markdown, legitimate reference URLs to accessibility resources, relative paths in docs, and false positives for 'key' (keyboard) and 'cryptographic' (MDN). No executable code, no shell commands, no malicious patterns exist.

content.value_statement: Web applications often exclude users with disabilities due to poor accessibility implementation. This skill provides WCAG 2.1 guidance for creating inclusive, screen-reader-friendly, keyboard-navigable interfaces.
actual_capabilities: Generate semantic HTML structures following WCAG 2.1 guidelines
Implement keyboard navigation patterns for modals, dropdowns, and tabs
Add proper ARIA attributes for screen reader compatibility
Create accessible forms with proper labels and error handling
Set up accessibility testing with axe-core and Jest
Ensure color contrast meets WCAG AA and AAA requirements
limitations: Does not perform live accessibility audits on existing websites
Cannot test with actual screen readers (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver)
Does not provide legal compliance certification
Framework-specific examples are limited to React, Vue, and vanilla JS

use_cases.title: Build New Accessible Components
use_cases.description: Create React modals, dropdowns, or forms with proper focus management, ARIA labels, and keyboard support from the start.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers building new UI components

use_cases.title: Fix Existing Accessibility Issues
use_cases.description: Identify and fix accessibility problems in legacy code by following WCAG checklists and implementing proper semantic HTML.
use_cases.target_user: Developers maintaining or auditing existing websites

use_cases.title: Implement Accessibility Testing
use_cases.description: Set up automated accessibility testing with axe-core in Jest to catch regressions before deployment.
use_cases.target_user: QA engineers and developers integrating accessibility into CI/CD

prompt_templates.title: Create Accessible Modal
prompt_templates.prompt:
Create an accessible modal component with these requirements:
- Framework: [React/Vue/Vanilla JS]
- WCAG Level: AA
- Features: Focus trap, ESC to close, click outside to close
- Include proper ARIA attributes and screen reader announcements

prompt_templates.title: Build Accessible Form
prompt_templates.prompt:
Build an accessible form with:
- Required field validation
- Clear error messages with aria-describedby
- Proper label associations
- Success/error state announcements
- Focus management on validation errors

prompt_templates.title: Add Keyboard Navigation
prompt_templates.prompt:
Add full keyboard navigation support to [dropdown/tabs/menu] including:
- Arrow key navigation
- Home/End key support
- Enter/Space activation
- Escape to close
- Visible focus indicators

prompt_templates.title: Accessibility Code Review
prompt_templates.prompt:
Review this component code for accessibility issues:
[PASTE CODE]
Check for: semantic HTML, ARIA usage, keyboard accessibility, color contrast, form labels, focus management

output_examples.input: Create an accessible modal in React
output: Returns a Modal component with role='dialog', aria-modal='true', aria-labelledby, focus trap, ESC handler, and click-outside-to-close
Includes accessibility checklist: semantic HTML, keyboard navigation, screen reader announcements
Form with <label htmlFor> associations, aria-required, aria-invalid, aria-describedby for error messages
Includes role='alert' for error announcements and proper focus management on validation failure

best_practices: Use semantic HTML elements first before adding ARIA attributes - native HTML elements have built-in accessibility
Always maintain visible focus indicators - never use outline: none without providing an alternative
Test with actual keyboard navigation and screen readers during development, not just automated tools
anti_patterns: Using div with role='button' instead of native <button> element
Using placeholder as the only label for form inputs
Removing outline:focus styles without providing visible focus indicators

faq.question: What is WCAG?
faq.answer: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) is a set of recommendations for making web content accessible to people with disabilities. It defines three levels: A, AA, and AAA.

faq.question: What is ARIA?
faq.answer: Accessible Rich Internet Applications (ARIA) is a set of attributes that define ways to make web content more accessible. It supplements native HTML semantics.

faq.question: What is a screen reader?
faq.answer: Screen readers are software applications that convert visual content into speech or Braille for users who are blind or visually impaired. Common ones include NVDA, JAWS, and VoiceOver.

faq.question: What is keyboard accessibility?
faq.answer: Keyboard accessibility means all website functions can be performed using only the keyboard (Tab, Enter, Space, Arrow keys, Escape). This helps users who cannot use a mouse.

faq.question: What is focus trapping?
faq.answer: Focus trapping keeps keyboard focus inside a modal or dialog until it is closed. This prevents users from tabbing to background content while the modal is open.

faq.question: How do I test accessibility?
faq.answer: Use automated tools like axe-core or Lighthouse for basic checks. Manual testing with keyboard only and screen readers is essential for comprehensive evaluation.
